fundamentals.name: Airbnb, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Airbnb, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, operates a platform for stays, experiences, and services worldwide. The company's marketplace connects hosts and guests online or through mobile devices to book spaces, experiences, and services. It also offers gift cards. The company was formerly known as AirBed & Breakfast, Inc. and changed its name to Airbnb, Inc. in November 2010. Airbnb, Inc. was founded in 2007 and is headquartered in San Francisco, California.

judgment.summary: Strong growth and profitability metrics are tempered by a high valuation and some operational margin concerns.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates; next earnings date is 2026-08-05.
positives: Revenue growth 17.9% YoY, with analyst estimates for current FY at 25.2%.
Profit margin 19.9% and Return on Equity 32.3% indicate strong profitability.
Free cash flow of $3.19B TTM supports operations and growth.
Forward P/E of 22.22x is significantly lower than trailing P/E of 33.16x, suggesting expected earnings growth.
Strong analyst consensus with a mean target of $156.51.
negatives: Operating margin of 3.2% is notably lower than the gross margin.
Quarterly earnings growth was only 3.9%, and the last reported quarter missed EPS estimates.
Price/Sales ratio of 6.30x and Price/Book of 10.47x suggest a premium valuation.
Quick ratio of 0.74 indicates potential short-term liquidity challenges.

news.paragraph: Airbnb is expanding its offerings by backing WeRoad to tap into the curated group travel market and has recently added grocery orders and hotels to its platform. While the company is attracting investor attention with these strategic moves, it also faces scrutiny regarding its valuation and growth trajectory compared to peers. The recent addition of new services aims to diversify revenue streams and capture new customer segments.
